request.question: Who was the director of the movie Home which was an adaptation of the book The true Meaning of Smekday?

title: True meaning of Christmas
Home (2015 film)
Home (soundtrack)
Órlaith
Eckhart (TV series)
Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls
Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh
Baby Looney Tunes' Eggs-traordinary Adventure
The True Meaning of Smekday
The Ragamuffin Gospel
sentences: The "true meaning of Christmas" is a phrase with a long history in American pop culture.
It first appears in the mid-19th century, and is often given vaguely religious overtones, suggesting that the "true meaning of Christmas" is the celebration of the Nativity of Christ.

But in pop culture usage, overt religious references are mostly avoided, and the "true meaning" is taken to be a sort of introspective and benevolent attitude as opposed to the commercialization of Christmas which has been lamented since at least the 1850s.
The poem "A Visit From St. Nicholas" (1822) helped popularize the tradition of exchanging gifts, and seasonal Christmas shopping began to assume economic importance.
Harriet Beecher Stowe criticizes the commercialization of Christmas in her story "Christmas; or, the Good Fairy".
An early expression of this sentiment using the phrase of "the true meaning" is found in "The American magazine", vol.
Home is a 2015 American 3D computer-animated science fiction comedy-adventure film produced by DreamWorks Animation and distributed by 20th Century Fox.
It is loosely based on Adam Rex's 2007 children's book "The True Meaning of Smekday" and starring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin, Jennifer Lopez, and Matt Jones.
Tim Johnson is the director of the film, Chris Jenkins and Suzanne Buirgy are its producers, and the screenplay is by Tom J. Astle and Matt Ember.
The story takes place on planet Earth, where an alien race called the Boov invade the planet.
However, a girl named Gratuity "Tip" Tucci manages to avoid capture, and goes on the run with Oh, a fugitive Boov.
Home: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is the soundtrack album for "Home", a 2015 animation film based on the 2007 children book "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.
It features songs recorded by Rihanna, Clarence Coffee Jr., Kiesza, Charli XCX, Jacob Plant, and Jennifer Lopez.
It was released on March 23, 2015 through Westbury Road and Roc Nation.
Following the announcement that Rihanna would star in the film, it was revealed she would release a concept album based on the animated film.
As the executive producer of the soundtrack, she called on various artists to feature on the album.
Rihanna's "Towards the Sun" and Jennifer Lopez's "Feel the Light" were released as singles to promote the album.
Órlaith (older spelling: Órfhlaith, newer spelling: Órla) is an Irish language female given name.
Orlagh is a hybrid spelling based on a merger with another suffix.

The meaning of the name dervies from Ór, meaning "Golden" and Flaith, meaning "prince" although as names with "flaith" suffixes are almost always exclusively feminine, this is usually interpreted as meaning "princess".
The retention of the 'fh' within the spelling maintains the ability to easily derive the true meaning of the name, but as this is silent, it is common to see this redacted.
All spellings of the name are however pronounced the same, as "OR-la".
Eckhart is a Canadian animated series presented on Teletoon Canada in 2000 and 2001.
It was created by David Weale and incorporates some of the sounds and culture of Weale's home province of Prince Edward Island in Canada.
Eckhart, the title character, was a mouse who was a character in a children's book by David Weale titled "The True Meaning of Crumbfest", which was also a Christmas special program on television.
Approximately 39 episodes of the program were produced.
It also was packaged for sale as a retail DVD product (26 episodes + the Christmas Special).
The program was broadcast in 25 countries around the world.
Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls is a book was written by Lawrence Schiffman, published in 1994 by Doubleday, as part of the Anchor Research Library.
The book's aim was to explain the true meaning of the Dead Sea Scrolls for Judaism and Christianity.
Previous to the publication of the book, many exaggerated and irresponsible claims about the scrolls were published.
"Reclaiming the Dead Sea Scrolls" "sets before the public the real Dead Sea Scrolls."
Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh is an American animated television series produced by DreamWorks Animation and animated by Titmouse, which debuted in July 2016 as a Netflix original series.
It was developed by Ryan Crego and Thurop Van Orman based on the 2015 film "Home", which in turn was based on the 2007 novel "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.
Baby Looney Tunes' Eggs-traordinary Adventure is a 2003 American direct-to-video animated comedy film from Warner Bros.
Animation.
It is the first, and only, movie based on the TV series "Baby Looney Tunes".
The film follows the main characters from "Baby Looney Tunes" as they go on a search for the true meaning of Easter.
The True Meaning of Smekday is a 2007 children's book by Adam Rex that was highly recommended by "The New York Times".
The book was adapted into the 2015 animated feature film "Home".
The Ragamuffin Gospel is a book about the essence of Christianity by former Franciscan priest Brennan Manning.
Manning argues that Jesus' gospel was one of grace, and that efforts to earn salvation are impossibly misguided.

He states that the true meaning of God's grace has been lost in society amidst a constant search to merely please God, as though the Almighty is only a "small minded book keeper," who tallies sins and uses them against humanity.
Citing numerous biblical references and utilizing colleagues' stories, Manning illustrates the simple need for humanity to accept the freedom of God's grace, and its power to change lives.
A popular quote from the book: “To evangelize a person is to say to him or her: you, too, are loved by God in the Lord Jesus.”
It was first published in 1990.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

response.hop1_query: Who was the director of the movie Home which was an adaptation of the book The true Meaning of Smekday?
hop1_docs: The True Meaning of Smekday | The True Meaning of Smekday is a 2007 children's book by Adam Rex that was highly recommended by "The New York Times".  The book was adapted into the 2015 animated feature film "Home".

Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh | Home: Adventures with Tip & Oh is an American animated television series produced by DreamWorks Animation and animated by Titmouse, which debuted in July 2016 as a Netflix original series.  It was developed by Ryan Crego and Thurop Van Orman based on the 2015 film "Home", which in turn was based on the 2007 novel "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.

Home (2015 film) | Home is a 2015 American 3D computer-animated science fiction comedy-adventure film produced by DreamWorks Animation and distributed by 20th Century Fox.  It is loosely based on Adam Rex's 2007 children's book "The True Meaning of Smekday" and starring the voices of Jim Parsons, Rihanna, Steve Martin, Jennifer Lopez, and Matt Jones.  Tim Johnson is the director of the film, Chris Jenkins and Suzanne Buirgy are its producers, and the screenplay is by Tom J. Astle and Matt Ember.  The story takes place on planet Earth, where an alien race called the Boov invade the planet.  However, a girl named Gratuity "Tip" Tucci manages to avoid capture, and goes on the run with Oh, a fugitive Boov.

Home (soundtrack) | Home: Original Motion Picture Soundtrack is the soundtrack album for "Home", a 2015 animation film based on the 2007 children book "The True Meaning of Smekday" by Adam Rex.  It features songs recorded by Rihanna, Clarence Coffee Jr., Kiesza, Charli XCX, Jacob Plant, and Jennifer Lopez.  It was released on March 23, 2015 through Westbury Road and Roc Nation.  Following the announcement that Rihanna would star in the film, it was revealed she would release a concept album based on the animated film.  As the executive producer of the soundtrack, she called on various artists to feature on the album.  Rihanna's "Towards the Sun" and Jennifer Lopez's "Feel the Light" were released as singles to promote the album.

If Only It Were True | If Only It Were True (original title: Et si c'etait vrai...) is French author Marc Levy's first novel, released in 1999.  It was translated into English in 2000 and was adapted into the American film "Just Like Heaven" in 2005.  A 2006 Bollywood movie named "I See You" is also an adaptation of this book.  2004 Malayalam movie "Vismayathumbathu" was also based on this book.

Marshall Pugh | Marshall Pugh (born 1925) is a British journalist and author.  He wrote a book called "Commander Crabb" based on the true story of a British officer who learned deep sea diving to thwart Italian frogmen who were sabotaging British naval forces.  He later adapted it into a movie called "The Silent Enemy" which was released in 1958.

Ömer Lütfi Akad | Ömer Lütfi Akad (2 September 1916 – 19 November 2011), was a Turkish film director, who directed movies from 1948 to 1974.  In 1949, he debuted as a film director with "Vurun Kahpeye" ("Strike the Whore") an adaptation of Halide Edip Adıvar's book of the same title.  He became one of the pioneers of the period in the "Director Generation".  The 1970s trilogy, "The Bride; The Wedding; and The Sacrifice", is considered his masterpiece.  Afterwards, he withdrew from movie making instead directing adaptations for TV.
response.summary_1:
- [1] quoted: "adapted into the 2015 animated feature film 'Home'."  
- [3] quoted: "Tim Johnson is the director of the film" (Home, 2015).  
- Bridge entity: Home (2015 film) — present and unambiguous in passages [1] and [3].  
- Missing/ambiguous facts: none for answering who directed the film.  
- Final answer (supported): Director of Home (film based on The True Meaning of Smekday) — Tim Johnson. [source: [3] "Tim Johnson is the director of the film"]
hop2_docs: Jason Wood (writer) | Jason Wood (born 1969) is a British writer on film and cinema.  He is currently the Artistic Director for Film at HOME, the centre for international contemporary arts, theatre and film formed by the merger of Cornerhouse and the Library Theatre Company. He was formerly Director of Programming at Curzon Cinemas from 2009 to 2015.

Michael Campus | Michael Campus (March 28, 1935 – May 15, 2015) was an American director, screenwriter and producer.  He is best known for directing the 1973 film "The Mack".  He died on May 15, 2015, at his home in Encino, California of melanoma.

Donald Wrye | Donald Wrye (September 24, 1934 – May 15, 2015) was an American director, screenwriter and producer.  He is best known for directing the 1978 film "Ice Castles".  He died on May 15, 2015, at his home in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania.

Mark Duplass | Mark David Duplass (born December 7, 1976) is an American film director, film producer, actor, musician, and screenwriter.  He is the brother of director, writer, and actor Jay Duplass.  Together, the brothers started the film production company Duplass Brothers Productions, and have directed films such as "The Puffy Chair" (2005), "Jeff, Who Lives at Home" (2011), and "The Do-Deca-Pentathlon" (2012).  Mark and Jay co-created the HBO television series "Togetherness" in 2015.

Looney Tunes: Rabbits Run | Looney Tunes: Rabbits Run is a 2015 American animated direct-to-video film based on "The Looney Tunes Show", produced by Warner Bros.  Animation.  It is the first new "Looney Tunes" direct-to-video film in nine years since "Bah, Humduck!  A Looney Tunes Christmas".  The film was directed by Jeff Siergey, a supervising animator on "Space Jam" and lead animator on "".  He was also a director on "The Looney Tunes Show".  It was released on August 4, 2015 by Warner Home Video, but it was released early on July 7, 2015 on Vudu and Walmart.

Rhaatee | Rhaatee (Kannada: ರಾಟೆ ) is a 2015 Indian Kannada romantic film written and directed by A. P. Arjun, in his third directorial venture after the successful films like "Ambari" and "Addhuri".  The film also marks the second production of music director V. Harikrishna under his home banner "Harmonium Reeds".  The film stars Dhananjay and Shruthi Hariharan in the lead roles.  The film has the musical score and soundtrack composed by V. Harikrishna.  The film, launched officially on 3 March 2013 underwent several delays and took a long shoot time and finally released theatrically on 20 March 2015.

North Circular Road (film) | North Circular Road is a 2015 feature film, and the debut of Irish writer and director Donal Nugent.  It tells the story of Janice Cadogan, a weather forecaster who begins to see ghostly apparitions in her new home on Dublin's North Circular Road.
response.summary_2: Tim Johnson is the director of the 2015 animated film Home, which was adapted from the book The True Meaning of Smekday.
